skill.name: co-tank-monitor
skill.description: IoT monitoring simulation to predict CO2 tank depletion and prevent weekend gas outages in cell culture facilities. Monitors cylinder pressure, calculates consumption rates, provides early warnings, and supports automated scheduling via cron.
skill.summary: Predict CO2 cylinder depletion and prevent weekend gas outages in cell culture labs.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 42 static findings across 3 files (628 total lines). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 17 weak cryptographic algorithm findings are scanner hallucinations on a JSON data file and function definitions containing no crypto code. The 21 shell backtick execution findings are markdown code block documentation showing CLI usage and cron configuration examples. The 4 hardcoded URL findings are legitimate reference links. The co2-tank-monitor skill is a legitimate lab monitoring simulation tool graded Production Ready by peer evaluation. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Backtick Execution Patterns in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 21 instances of shell backtick syntax in SKILL.md. EVALUATION: FALSE POSITIVE. All instances are in markdown code blocks showing CLI usage, cron configurations, and Python code samples. The skill intentionally runs scripts/main.py via shell execution as its documented purpose. No code injection or user-controlled input is present in the commands. These are safe documentation examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 17 instances of weak cryptographic algorithms across 3 files. EVALUATION: FALSE POSITIVE. The files contain no cryptographic code. scripts/main.py performs only arithmetic division for pressure calculations. The JSON audit file contains evaluation scores and assertions. SKILL.md is documentation. Scanner hallucinated on non-cryptographic identifiers.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URL References in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 4 hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md. EVALUATION: FALSE POSITIVE. These are legitimate reference URLs: the GitHub source repository link, ThermoFisher cell culture guidelines, and OSHA gas cylinder safety page. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network calls present. The skill does not make any network requests at runtime.

content.user_title: Monitor CO2 Tank Levels and Prevent Weekend Gas Outages
content.value_statement: Cell culture facilities risk losing expensive samples during weekend gas outages when laboratories are unmanned. This skill monitors CO2 cylinder pressure, predicts depletion times, and provides early warnings to prevent gas supply failures.
actual_capabilities: Predicts CO2 cylinder depletion time based on current pressure and daily consumption rate
Detects weekend depletion risk and provides color-coded status alerts with recommendations
Supports 10L and 40L cylinder sizes with automatic unit conversion from PSI and Bar to MPa
Generates random simulation scenarios for staff training without requiring real sensor data
Provides machine-readable status codes for integration with automated workflows and cron jobs
limitations: Predictions assume constant consumption rate; actual usage may vary with temperature and usage patterns
CLI supports single cylinder per run; multi-cylinder monitoring requires running separately
Does not connect to physical sensors; requires manual pressure readings as input
No historical data tracking; each run is an independent calculation without trend analysis

use_cases.title: Weekly CO2 Tank Status Check
use_cases.description: Lab technicians check cylinder pressure each morning and input readings to predict when tanks need replacement before they run out.

use_cases.title: Pre-Weekend Gas Outage Prevention
use_cases.description: Facility managers run pre-weekend checks on Friday to identify tanks at risk of depleting during unmanned weekend periods.

use_cases.title: Staff Training with Simulation Mode
use_cases.description: New lab staff practice monitoring procedures using simulated sensor data without requiring real cylinder readings.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Tank Status Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check CO2 tank status with pressure 8.5 MPa and daily consumption 1.2 MPa per day.
prompt_templates.scenario: Daily morning check for routine monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Weekend Risk Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a pre-weekend check with current pressure 5.5 MPa, daily consumption 1.5 MPa per day, and alert threshold of 3 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: Friday afternoon check before an unmanned weekend

prompt_templates.title: Pressure Unit Conversion and Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Check tank status. The gauge reads 1200 PSI and daily consumption is approximately 1.2 MPa per day.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reading pressure from equipment using non-standard units

prompt_templates.title: Training Simulation Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a training scenario and walk me through the complete monitoring procedure as if training a new lab assistant.
prompt_templates.scenario: Staff training on CO2 monitoring procedures

output_examples.input: Check CO2 tank status with pressure 8.5 MPa and daily consumption 1.2 MPa per day.
output_examples.output:
CO2 Tank Monitor Report:
- Current Pressure: 8.50 MPa
- Daily Consumption: 1.20 MPa/day
- Tank Capacity: 40 L
- Remaining Days: 7.1 days
- Depletion Time: Thursday, June 4, 2026
- Weekend Risk: None detected
- Status: Normal (Green)
- Recommendation: No immediate action required. Next check in 5 days.

output_examples.input: Check tank pressure of 3.5 MPa with daily consumption 1.5 MPa per day on a Friday afternoon.
output_examples.output:
CO2 Tank Monitor Report:
- Current Pressure: 3.50 MPa
- Daily Consumption: 1.50 MPa/day
- Tank Capacity: 40 L
- Remaining Days: 2.3 days
- Depletion Time: Sunday evening
- Weekend Risk: High depletion during Saturday or Sunday
- Status: Danger (Red)
- Recommendation: Replace tank immediately or arrange weekend monitoring duty.

best_practices: Take pressure readings at the same time each day, ideally within 30 minutes of 9:00 AM.
Calculate daily consumption rate from actual usage data over at least two weeks.
Standardize all pressure readings to MPa before input; clearly document when PSI or Bar conversion is applied.
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate pressure readings or depletion predictions when input data is missing.
Do not use this skill for monitoring gases other than CO2 or for managing incubator temperature.
Do not ignore the constant consumption rate constraint; actual depletion may vary significantly.

faq.question: What pressure units does this skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts MPa, PSI, and Bar. PSI and Bar values are auto-converted to MPa with an explicit assumption stated in the output.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to physical CO2 sensors?
faq.answer: No. This is a calculation tool that requires manual pressure readings. For automated sensor integration, use the cron examples provided in the documentation.

faq.question: How accurate are the depletion predictions?
faq.answer: Predictions assume a constant consumption rate. Accuracy depends on consistent daily usage patterns. Calculate your consumption rate from actual data over two weeks.

faq.question: Can I monitor multiple cylinders at the same time?
faq.answer: The CLI supports one cylinder per run. For multiple cylinders, run the tool separately for each cylinder with its own pressure and consumption values.

faq.question: What is the simulation mode for?
faq.answer: Simulation mode generates random training scenarios with realistic pressure and consumption values. Use it for staff training without real sensor data.

faq.question: How do I set up automated daily checks?
faq.answer: Use the cron examples in the documentation. The quiet mode flag suppresses verbose output for automated scripts and returns a machine-readable status code.
